steps.title: Clear the denominator
steps.content: Multiply both sides of $u=-\dfrac{w}{164x}$ by $164x$:$$164x\cdot u=-w$$

steps.title: Compare with each choice
steps.content:
• A) $w=-164ux$: matches the derived expression.
  • B) $w=-\dfrac{164u}{x}$: would require dividing by $x$, but we multiplied by $164x$. Eliminated.
  • C) $w=\dfrac{x}{164u}$: wrong variable placement and wrong sign. Eliminated.
  • D) $w=u+164x$: the original equation is multiplicative, not additive. Eliminated.
A is correct.
